pr.title: Match duplicated IASA and Phys functions in ftCrazyhand
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched a large batch of duplicated Crazy Hand IASA and Phys action callbacks in src/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init.c. The PR replaces many /// # placeholders with simple repeated bodies: IASA callbacks fetch the Fighter from the HSD_GObj and, for player slot type 0, call ftBossLib_8015BD20; Phys callbacks generally forward to ft_80085134. decomp.dev reported 68 new matches and +3724 matched bytes.
what_changed: Added concrete implementations for numerous ftCh_*_IASA callbacks that follow the same pattern: `Fighter* ft = GET_FIGHTER(gobj); if (Player_GetPlayerSlotType(ft->player_id) == 0) { ftBossLib_8015BD20(gobj); }`.
Added concrete implementations for many ftCh_*_Phys callbacks that simply call `ft_80085134(gobj);`.
Left unrelated Anim and Coll callbacks as existing placeholders or empty functions where not part of this match batch.
Added include dependencies needed by the newly matched callbacks: `baselib/forward.h`, `ft/ft_081B.h`, and `pl/player.h`.

Affected Crazy Hand action states include Wait1_0, Entry, Damage, WaitSweep, SweepLoop, SweepWait, Walk2, WalkLoop, WalkShoot, PaperCrush, Poke1, Poke2, FingerBeamStart/End, BackAirplane2/3, BackCrush, Grab, Squeezing, Squeeze, Throw, Slam, Fail, TagCrush, and many IASA-only states.
smart_moves: Recognized that many Crazy Hand IASA callbacks were byte-identical duplicates and matched them by repeating the small body per symbol rather than abstracting into a shared helper.
Recognized the repeated Phys callback pattern as a direct wrapper around `ft_80085134(gobj)`, allowing many functions to be matched with minimal risk.
Preserved the action callback naming layout in place, replacing only the specific `/// #ftCh_*_IASA` and `/// #ftCh_*_Phys` placeholders with implementations.
Kept function bodies intentionally simple, which is important for matching short duplicated callbacks where extra locals, helper calls, or refactors can perturb codegen.
Added the exact headers required for `GET_FIGHTER`, `Player_GetPlayerSlotType`, `ft_80085134`, and `HSD_GObj` usage instead of relying on accidental transitive declarations.
decomp_lessons: For boss fighter action tables, scan for repeated IASA and Phys callback patterns across many action states; one confirmed body can unlock many placeholder functions.
Duplicated functions should usually be emitted as duplicated C bodies when each symbol must independently match; factoring into a shared helper may be cleaner source but can destroy matching.
IASA callbacks in this ftCrazyHand slice commonly gate boss AI/input work on `Player_GetPlayerSlotType(ft->player_id) == 0` before calling `ftBossLib_8015BD20(gobj)`.
Simple Phys callbacks in this slice commonly forward to `ft_80085134(gobj)` with no additional local state.
Small callback matches can produce significant aggregate progress: the bot reported 68 new function matches and +3724 matched bytes from one file.
naming_conventions: Crazy Hand functions use the `ftCh_` prefix.
Action callback suffixes follow the project pattern `_Anim`, `_IASA`, `_Phys`, and `_Coll`.
The matched callbacks retain state names in the middle of the symbol, e.g. `ftCh_Wait1_0_IASA`, `ftCh_FingerBeamStart_Phys`, `ftCh_TagGrab_IASA`.
The file path and unit naming use `ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init`, matching the `ftCh_` symbol prefix.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace `/// #function` nonmatching placeholders with exact minimal C wrappers for short callbacks.

For the IASA duplicate body, use `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` into a `Fighter*` local, read `ft->player_id`, compare `Player_GetPlayerSlotType(...)` to integer 0, and call `ftBossLib_8015BD20(gobj)` only on that branch.
For the Phys duplicate body, emit only `ft_80085134(gobj);`.
Do not combine duplicate callbacks into a macro or helper if the goal is per-function codegen matching.
Header fixes were part of the matching tactic: `ft/ft_081B.h` for `ft_80085134`, `pl/player.h` for `Player_GetPlayerSlotType`, and `baselib/forward.h` for forward declarations.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported for GALE01: matched code increased to 34.61%, +0.10%, +3724 bytes.
decomp.dev bot reported 68 new matches in `main/melee/ft/chara/ftCrazyHand/ftCh_Init`, with listed IASA examples moving from 0.00% to 100.00%.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining `/// #ftCh_*_IASA` placeholders in ftCrazyHand that may use the same `Player_GetPlayerSlotType` / `ftBossLib_8015BD20` pattern.
Search for remaining `/// #ftCh_*_Phys` placeholders in ftCrazyHand that may be direct `ft_80085134(gobj)` wrappers.
Compare ftCrazyHand IASA and Phys callbacks against ftMasterHand equivalents for shared boss callback patterns.
Audit other boss fighter files for many short duplicated action callbacks that can be matched in batches.
